Transcrição Completa: 51 The Educator Sales Role 1 31 25
[00:00]
"So, one problem that well, it's it's a problem solution scenario that we faced recently is there's two types of people that show up to these sales calls nowadays coming from VSSL funnels."
[00:15]
"And in some instances, as scary as it is, coming through like webinars and challenge funnels, but you'll mainly see this type of person that we're going to address and the role and the solution that you need in order in order to sell to this type of person from direct call bookings, from hydra lead forms."
[00:30]
"So allow me to first just articulate these two types of people. Okay. So there's person number one and then there's person number two."
[00:38] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Person Number One"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Representação visual de perfil de cliente tipo 1, características de comportamento de consumo de conteúdo
[00:40]
"Person number one, they are happy and enthused to go through all your framing material. So they they love to go through framing. They're happy to like watch your pre-call email sequence videos you send to them."
[00:52]
"They prefer long form. They love going through things first that are essentially going to sell them that getting on the call is a good idea."
[01:00]
"All right, this type of person, they love like lengthy framing based funnels like webinars, challenge funnels, you know, all the all that kind of fun stuff."
[01:08]
"And again, if they do come through a call funnel, they'll consume every bit and then some of your pre-call email sequencing."
[01:15]
"On top of that, this this is actually kind of crazy, too. They'll research the shit out of you and go to your YouTube and like binge watch everything that you've got, whether it's long form, short form, like combination of all of it."
[01:27]
"That's what they prefer. Like person number one loves the framing before they get to the sales conversation."
[01:33]
"And your sales people absolutely love person number one. Okay, your salespeople, this is what they call qualified."
[01:41] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Qualified = Layup Deal"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Comparação visual entre "pessoa qualificada" e "negócio fácil", metáfora de caixa de supermercado, processo de vendas simplificado
[01:43]
"Okay, because these are perceptually like a layup deal comparatively to person number two."
[01:49]
"Okay, person number one, as a result of going through all the framing material, as I'm sure you're starting to conclude yourself, would be a more ideal person for your sales team to talk to."
[02:00]
"It would require you as an organization to do far less sales training because your sales people are just essentially cashiers at a grocery store at this point."
[02:08]
"All they're doing all day long is just talking to layup deals if you only get person number one through."
[02:13]
"Now, the illusion, and this is this is a really unfortunate thing that some businesses try to convince themselves of is that person number two is somehow unqualified comparatively to person number one. And that's just not the case."
[02:27] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Person Number Two"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Representação de perfil de cliente tipo 2, ênfase em valorização de tempo, comportamento de consumo diferenciado
[02:29]
"So, person number two values time a lot more than person number one."
[02:34]
"So, here's the thing. Like, person number one in their long-form consumption can find something that kind of trips them up and makes them say, 'Ah, I don't really know if this is for me.'"
[02:45]
"And that's what person number one wants. Person number one is trying to trust you, but they're also looking for things and reasons not to do it, but ideally the content validates them."
[02:54]
"So by the time they get on the phones or by the time they show up for the conversion mechanism, they're ready to buy."
[03:00]
"Person number two is the opposite. When I say they value time, it's not that person number one doesn't value time. It's just person number two, they like in their own mind, they're like, I'm not going to fucking watch all these videos this guy's got."
[03:14]
"I'm not going to consume all this stuff that this person's got. They would never register for a webinar. They would never in any fucking lifetime they ever live go through a challenge funnel or all your pre-call email sequencing. No. No."
[03:27] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Person #2 Wants to Feel Special"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Conceito de exclusividade, preferência por comunicação one-on-one, rejeição de conteúdo em massa
[03:29]
"Person number two, I like to say they want to feel kind of special. That's a that's a good way to put it."
[03:35]
"They will prefer to get information on that sales call that would otherwise and could have been consumed through pre-call framing material. Okay."
[03:43]
"And as a result of that, because your salesperson then has to do an education call rather than a sales call, person number two gets deemed as unqualified."
[03:52] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Person #2 = Longer Sales Cycle"
NÚMEROS: 2 calls vs 1 call
VISUAL: Comparação de ciclos de venda, timeline de fechamento, primeiro call como educação
[03:54]
"Person number two generally has a longer sales cycle or at the least it's a two call close instead of a one call close due to the fact that the first call ends up being an education call."
[04:05]
"And here's the thing, like it's more of like a one-on-one communication preference. They'll still and this is this is the important thing to understand like they still want information. Okay?"
[04:16]
"So it's an illusion to think they don't like they still want all of the info. They just consume it differently."
[04:22]
"And here's the thing, some businesses are built to sell to only one or the other. And that's where the problem is."
[04:28] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "The Problem: Businesses Built for Only One Type"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Diagrama de negócios especializados, limitações de abordagem única, perda de oportunidades
[04:30]
"That's what this video is attempting to help you solve is, are you a business that's preferentially and built to only sell to person number one?"
[04:38]
"And then there's also a minority of businesses that are built to only sell to person number two."
[04:43]
"If you've got a business built to only sell to person number two, you generally are not very good with like long form content. Your indoctrination sequences are pretty weak."
[04:52]
"Um, you just you just don't really commit to like long form content at all. You're not really producing that many reels. You're more of like a direct response business."
[05:00]
"You're happy to just get leads that you can talk to, follow up with. Like that's your preference to sell to people."
[05:06]
"And as a result of that, you do a really good job with person number two. But as a result of being that way, you do an awful job with person number one because person number one, like they won't even jump on the phone with you because they want to make sure that it's worth their time to jump on the phone with you."
[05:22]
"As interesting as that is, okay, person number one will spend a lot more time before they get on the call, but that's their again, that's their buying preference. They want to do that. Okay."
[05:32] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Businesses Built for Person #1"
NÚMEROS: Low to mid couple hundred grand, maybe high couple hundred grand
VISUAL: Perfil de negócios orgânicos, transição para paid ads, faturamento em centenas de milhares
[05:34]
"And again, people who are built to sell to person number one exclusively, they're really good with long form content. These are typically the organic businesses that are trying to transition into paid."
[05:44]
"They've gotten to like low to mid couple hundred grand, maybe even high couple hundred grand, and they're trying to get into paid advertising, but they're kind of struggling with it because for the first time, they're having to get exposed to person number two archetypes. Okay?"
[06:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "THE SOLUTION: The Educator Role"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Novo papel na estrutura de vendas, posicionamento entre setters e closers, função educacional
[06:02]
"And this is as simple as it is. You have to have this role. And this this was what we attempted to do um in order to to essentially satiate a business being able to sell to both of these individuals."
[06:15]
"We introduced this role in the sales teams called the educator. Okay?"
[06:20]
"And the educator is a very simple job function in the sales organization. You know how you have setters and you have closers? Well, now you'll also have an educator as an additional role in the business. Okay."
[06:31] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Sales Team Structure: Setters → Closers → EDUCATORS"
NÚMEROS: 3 papéis principais
VISUAL: Organograma de vendas, fluxo de calls, introdução do terceiro papel
[06:33]
"Educators are very simple to understand in terms of their function. They're catered to selling to person number two."
[06:40]
"They're almost like, okay, let me give you some scenarios where the educator comes in. Okay, you don't just flow everybody to the educator."
[06:48]
"You're still going to follow your exact same processes that you have now where if somebody books a call like you send them to the setters, you send them to the closers, however you currently do it."
[06:57]
"However, if a person number two type of individual shows up on those calls, instead of your current setters and closers who just bitch about selling to that type of person, having to do the call like a batline, you hit up the educator and you live transfer the call to them. Okay?"
[07:14] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Live Transfer Process"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Processo de transferência ao vivo, transição de call no Zoom, handoff entre vendedor e educador
[07:16]
"So, if you're doing a Zoom call as an example, you would ping the educator that you have in the business and you would be like, 'Hey man, I need you to jump on this call real quick.'"
[07:25]
"You would bring that person onto the call and you would transition who leads the call and then the setter or the closer that was originally on the phone jumps off after the transition is successfully made and now the educator steps in."
[07:37]
"The educator is essentially doing when I say there's like a one-on-one communication preference. Okay. What's happening is is they're doing like the same thing."
[07:46]
"They're answering questions on a one-on-one level. They're helping the person work through the resources. They're helping the person get the answers that they need."
[07:54] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Educator = Custom Webinar in Real Time"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Conceito de webinar personalizado, sessão educacional one-on-one, resposta a perguntas específicas
[07:56]
"They're treating it almost like it's a it's like a custom webinar that's being done in real time."
[08:01]
"That's what person number two hates about webinars. They hate that they have to sit there and wait for you to get to what they actually want to know. And that's why they don't go to them."
[08:10]
"They hate that they have to watch your long form videos to get the answers that they need. They just want to know what fucking time in the video they need to go and watch. Okay."
[08:18] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Additional Solutions for Person #2"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Recursos complementares, time codes em vídeos, navegação facilitada de conteúdo
[08:20]
"Now, there's a few things that you can do besides hiring an educator to also help with this. If you add like, okay, if you go scrub any of my YouTube videos, all my YouTube videos have like the little time code things where as you're scrubbing through it, you can look at what topic is being discussed in that exact moment."
[08:37]
"You would want to do that same thing for your VSSLs, as an example, to successfully sell to person number two."
[08:43]
"For all of your pre-call indoctrination and email sequences, you would want to have it to where you break information down in a way where it's like this is talked about here, this is talked about here, this is talked about here, and it's like clickable to that section of the video that you're discussing in."
[08:58]
"Do you get what I'm saying? That's the best way to take the pre-call information or the pre-webinar information or whatever it is and get that person to actually consume it for person number two."
[09:08]
"But the educator role is extremely effective. It's essentially a setter like in its function."
[09:14] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Educator Function = Setter-Like"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Comparação de funções, papel de facilitação, transição para closer
[09:16]
"It's it's just a person that's on the phones that's helping the individual on the other end get their questions answered and then the educator occasionally controls the call and treats it like it's a sales call."
[09:27]
"Their sole function is generally to set the call to a closer that they discuss with next. You can also have that educator be a closer and set the call for themselves to close next."
[09:36]
"Because here's the thing, the educator is a type of salesperson that loves to deal with this type of individual. Okay?"
[09:43]
"It's not like they are upset that they have to talk to this person or do multiple calls. They're happy to do it. That's this person's job for a reason."
[09:51]
"Whereas your original sales team that you have now, they fucking hate person number two. And that's why they sell so poorly to those individuals comparatively. You get what I mean?"
[10:01] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Educator Role Summary"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Resumo de funções, expectativas do papel, estrutura de comissionamento
[10:03]
"So, okay. Anyway, long story short, the educator sales role, I encourage that you hire them."
[10:09]
"It's an expectations thing that you've got to work through where when you hire the individual, you're essentially trying to make sure you articulate it in this way where you're like, 'Look, your job is to get on the phone, go through like essentially decks and webinars and like all the resources of the company and help the individual get their questions answered and then just set a follow-up call for yourself to close or set a follow-up call to a closer. That's it. That's your function.'"
[10:34]
"And then you pay the person like you would a setter. So it's like the same commission structure that you currently have for your setters. That's what you would pay these individuals."
[10:42]
"And if they end up obviously setting the calls themselves, you would pay them like a closer too. Totally up to you for how you choose to execute it."
[10:49] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Test Results"
NÚMEROS: 0.5x to 1x additional ROAS bump
VISUAL: Resultados de testes iniciais, aumento de ROAS, mais fechamentos
[10:51]
"But it's been a recent test that we've run like a very recent test and we've seen a lot of success with it in terms of just getting more people closed and increasing the rorowaz a little bit."
[11:00]
"Like this generally can get about a .5 to a 1x additional bump in whatever your current rorowaz is because these types of people are coming through your funnel anyway."
[11:09]
"You always get both person number one and person number two. They'll all come through your funnels. They'll all come through especially the direct response funnels like call funnels and the hydra as an example."
[11:18]
"Why not have this role?"
[11:20] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Alternative Approach: Reframe Existing Sales Team"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Abordagem alternativa, treinamento de equipe existente, adaptação de habilidades
[11:22]
"There's another way to go about doing this by the way if you are a great sales manager. Okay? Or if you have great sales management in place, you do a reframe with the existing sales team where you teach them about these two types of people."
[11:35]
"And instead of and instead of you hiring an educator role, you just make your existing salespeople adapted in their skill sets to recognize when they have one person or the other."
[11:46] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "How to Identify Person #2"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Pergunta de qualificação, identificação rápida de tipo de cliente
[11:48]
"So, a good way to identify, to be clear, somebody shows up to the call, you just ask the question, 'Hey, did you get a chance to like watch any of the videos or anything that we were sending over to you in the emails or text prior to this?'"
[12:00]
"'Oh, no. I didn't get a chance yet.' Bingo. Person number two. Okay?"
[12:05]
"That's the simplest way to identify it. And it's the most consistent way to identify that person type. Okay?"
[12:11]
"And then from there, your salesperson that's on that call, they know it's like, 'All right, well, I want I'm here right now to get you as much information as I can to make this decision one way or the other.'"
[12:22] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Educator Script Framework"
NÚMEROS: 10-day timeline
VISUAL: Script de abordagem, framework de comunicação, timeline de decisão
[12:24]
"'Whether you're in or out, as easy as possible. Ideally, we can commit to about a 10-day time frame on getting you all the information you need to make a decision so you could go one way or the other.'"
[12:35]
"'Um, let's dive in. You know, what do you want to get started with? What is it that you want to know?'"
[12:40]
"And they'll generally ask some super fucking broad question to start the conversation. Something that will piss a normal salesperson off who's not framed on this type of individual."
[12:49]
"They'll say something super broad like, 'Yeah, so I mean, what what what is it?' you know, like 'how does this all work?'"
[12:56]
"And again, a normal salesperson that's not framed on this individual, they'll be like, 'Fuck this, dude. Like, this person's cold. They're way too early in the buying process.' And that's just not true. That's not true."
[13:08] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Person #2 Reality Check"
NÚMEROS: 2 call close, first call = education
VISUAL: Realidade vs percepção, qualificação verdadeira, timeline de fechamento
[13:10]
"They are at the point where it's typically a two call close. And they're on their first call. And instead of consuming the information like a person number one archetype, they're a person number two archetype, okay?"
[13:23]
"And that's just how they are. Don't treat them like they're unqualified. They're definitely qualified, but they vet their time, okay, being worth committing to the consumption of resources based on you telling them like what specifically they need to consume to get their answers."
[13:40]
"That's the intention of whether it's an educator doing the call or whether you're adapting your existing sales team to the call."
[13:47] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Handling Broad Questions"
NÚMEROS: 10-day decision timeline
VISUAL: Resposta a perguntas amplas, estratégia de curadoria de recursos
[13:49]
"They ask that super broad fucking 'Yeah. So, what is it like? How does it work?' Again, 'hey, this is what it is. You know, this is a little bit of how how it works.'"
[14:00]
"'Um, you know, tell me I mean, give me a list of questions that you got. You know, let me let me help you work through and navigate so you can save time. I'm going to make a list for you.'"
[14:10]
"'That's my goal of this call. I want to make a list for you of some resources and, you know, some some consumption material that can get you the answers you need so you can make that proper decision within our 10-day timeline here that we have a goal of you you getting to the point of a decision.'"
[14:26]
"'Either way, in or out, 10 days is our goal. I just want to curate a list of resources for you on this call together so I can get both your questions answered and get you some material to consume in between now and our next conversation. Does that sound fair? What do you got?'"
[14:42] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Resource Curation Strategy"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Processo de curadoria, lista personalizada de recursos, navegação de conteúdo existente
[14:44]
"You know, and again, all you're doing, man, you're just you're just looking through everything that you've built out and you're trying to curate a list that you hand off to this person. You see what I mean?"
[14:54]
"So, okay, long story short, whether it's an educator role or whether it is your existing sales team adapting to being able to sell to this individual type, that is what you have to do to close more deals. Okay."
[15:07] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "What This Solves"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Problemas resolvidos, negócios orgânicos transitando para paid, aumento de revenue e ROAS
[15:09]
"This is one of the things that holds back those organic businesses that try to transition into paid advertising. This is one thing that holds back the people who are doing paid advertising and not seeing as much ROZ as they want."
[15:21]
"They're throwing away opportunities to close these types of deals and it holds back their revenue and their rorowes as a result because they just constantly are saying, 'Oh no, this type of person sucks. Like I don't want to sell to that individual.' And it's just not true. It's just not true and it loses a lot of money as a result."
[15:39]
"So anyway, if you got any follow-up questions on this, feel free to let me know. Happy to give you any answers."
[15:45] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Testing Phase & Future Updates"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Fase de testes, promessa de atualizações futuras, convite para compartilhar resultados
[15:47]
"And then obviously as this role advances in its testing function to something that we just roll out and essentially mandate in every deal to do uh we'll come back and we'll update this video as well as a resource with more tips and insights that we found that have worked."
[16:02]
"And if you try this make sure you share some insights as well because again this is a very new thing that we're testing out."
[16:08]
"It's a new way to kind of solve like the VSSL problem is what some of the sales managers we work with have dubbed it."
[16:15]
"And uh again great great initial test results. Would love to see what some of you experience as results with the same thing."
📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO
NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS:
0.5x a 1x de aumento adicional no ROAS atual
Timeline de decisão: 10 dias
Estrutura de vendas: 3 papéis (Setters, Closers, Educators)
2 calls para fechar Person #2 vs 1 call para Person #1
Faturamento típico de negócios orgânicos: low to mid/high couple hundred grand (centenas de milhares)
ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:
Papel do Educator: Nova função na equipe de vendas especializada em Person #2
Live Transfer: Processo de transferência ao vivo de calls entre setter/closer para educator
Resource Curation: Curadoria personalizada de materiais educacionais durante a call
Time Codes: Implementação de marcadores de tempo em VSSLs e conteúdos longos
Pergunta de Qualificação: "Você assistiu aos vídeos/materiais que enviamos?" para identificar Person #2
Timeline de 10 dias: Framework de decisão estruturado para Person #2
Abordagem Alternativa: Treinar equipe existente para reconhecer e adaptar-se aos dois tipos
ESTRUTURA:
Person #1 (Consumidor de Conteúdo):
Adora framing material
Assiste todos os vídeos pré-call
Pesquisa extensivamente no YouTube
Prefere webinars e challenge funnels
Consome email sequences completas
"Layup deal" para vendedores
Fecha em 1 call
Person #2 (Valoriza Tempo):
Não consome conteúdo pré-call
Quer se sentir especial
Prefere comunicação one-on-one
Rejeita webinars e challenge funnels
Quer informação customizada na call
Fecha em 2 calls
Primeira call = educação
Estrutura de Comissionamento:
Educator pago como Setter
Se fechar própria call, pago também como Closer
Mesma estrutura de comissão dos setters existentes
DADOS FINANCEIROS:
Aumento de 0.5x a 1x no ROAS atual
Aumento no revenue por não descartar Person #2
Negócios orgânicos faturando centenas de milhares tentando escalar com paid ads
Perda de dinheiro significativa ao ignorar Person #2
DURAÇÃO: ~16:20 minutos
FORMATO: Apresentação educacional em vídeo com slides explicativos sobre estratégia de vendas
